examples: That twenty-one ceramic dog figurines were discovered during the excavating of a 1,000-year-old Hohokam village in Tempe, Arizona, has nearly doubled the number of these artifacts known to exist.
Bordering the Anasazi culture in the north, a separate civilization emerged in southern Arizona, called the Hohokam.
The ability to divert water into small agricultural plots meant that the Hohokam could live in large agricultural communities of relatively high population density.
